name: Jill's Focaccia
recipeIngredient: 1 cup artichoke hearts or sliced mushrooms
recipeInstructions:
1. Make a slack dough in a bowl, electric mixer, or bread machine using the first 9 ingredients.
2. Add the olives and pine nuts just before the last 1/2 cup flour if hand kneading or using a mixer, or 5 minutes before the end of the bread machine cycle (to keep them whole).
3. Let the dough double in size for about 1 1/2 hours.
4. Punch down the dough and transfer it to a cornmeal-dusted, heavily dusted 14-inch round pizza pan.
5. Make indentations in the dough with your finger tips.
6. Cover and let rise for 45 minutes or until doubled and puffy.
7. Push vegetables of choice into the dough to keep them anchored down.
8. Preheat the oven to 500°F, then turn it down to 450°F.
9. Bake foccacia for 15 to 20 minutes or until golden brown.
10. Remove from the oven and sprinkle with fresh basil and cracked black pepper.
11. Fresh tomato slices, chopped garlic, and a bit of olive oil enhance this bread to sublime perfection.
Note: Italian flour has the lowest ash content, protein level at 9.8%, and is used to add lightness to pizza and other dishes. Diastatic malt powder (or barley malt syrup) helps produce a finer texture and longer keeping quality when added to dough.
